creator: zarychtam@plan-b.pwste.edu.pl
text:
Now both: bhyve host and guest are running FreeBSD stable/14. ZFS pools on host and guest were upgraded. The report is still valid and the issue is persistent.  Maybe the transition to mirror (the guest is nested on a ZVOL of pool with a top-level-vdev that had previously been removed) has something to do with it (see bug 273663). The guest is using host's ZVOL zroot/ZVOL/virtbsd as vtbd0.




This is how ZFS looks on the host: 

# zpool status zroot
  pool: zroot
 state: ONLINE
  scan: scrub repaired 0B in 00:11:06 with 0 errors on Fri Jan 26 03:21:55 2024
    scan warning: skipped blocks that are only referenced by the checkpoint.
remove: Removal of vdev 1 copied 15.1G in 0h0m, completed on Fri May 15 20:04:34 2020
	119K memory used for removed device mappings
checkpoint: created Mon Dec  4 20:10:12 2023, consumes 49.6G
config:

	NAME          STATE     READ WRITE CKSUM
	zroot         ONLINE       0     0     0
	  mirror-0    ONLINE       0     0     0
	    da0p2     ONLINE       0     0     0
	    da1p2     ONLINE       0     0     0

errors: No known data errors
